title: Some Nights Your Mind Won't Settle 🌙 Raag Chandrakauns 741Hz | Veena & Tabla for Deep Work
title_options: Some Nights Your Mind Won't Settle 🌙 Raag Chandrakauns 741Hz | Veena & Tabla for Deep Work
Mental Clarity Now ✨ Raag Chandrakauns 741Hz | Veena & Tabla for Deep Study Flow
For the Midnight Scholar 📖 Raag Chandrakauns 741Hz | Veena & Tabla for Deep Concentration
description:
Raag Chandrakauns 741Hz for focused work & clarity. Veena & tabla for deep study and mental stillness through sound.

Put the world down. For now, nothing is your problem.

0:00 Introduction — Setting the Intention
5:00 Alap — Free Exploration of Raag Chandrakauns
15:00 Vilambit — Slow & Deep Meditation
35:00 Madhya Laya — Deepening the Stillness
52:00 Samapti — Resolution & Inner Peace
58:00 Outro — Carry the Calm Forward

Raag Chandrakauns is a deeply introspective melody, ideal for the quiet hours of midnight. Its mysterious and meditative quality, with each deliberate note, fosters profound mental clarity. The 741Hz frequency further supports cognitive function by aiding in problem-solving and cleansing mental clutter, preparing your mind for deep work.

📖 PERFECT FOR:
- Late-night study sessions
- Uninterrupted deep work
- Dissolving mental blocks
- Enhancing problem-solving
- Finding creative flow
- Cultivating absolute stillness for thought

🎵 WHY RAAG CHANDRAKAUNS HEALS: The deliberate, grave notes of Raag Chandrakauns gently guide your mind into a parasympathetic state, reducing neural overactivity. This ancient sound architecture encourages focused attention, allowing you to access deeper cognitive reserves and achieve sustained concentration.

────────────────────────────

🇮🇳 हिंदी में:
राग चंद्रकौंस की रहस्यमय और गहरी धुनें आपके मन को आधी रात की शांति में ले जाती हैं। वीणा और तबला का सामंजस्य विचारों को शांत कर, आपको एकाग्रता और स्पष्टता प्रदान करता है। गहन अध्ययन और ध्यान केंद्रित करने के लिए यह संगीत अद्भुत है।
"दुनिया को किनारे रख दें। अभी के लिए, आपकी कोई समस्या नहीं है।"

────────────────────────────

💛 If this music brought you stillness, please LIKE, SUBSCRIBE, and SHARE with someone who needs peace.
🔔 Subscribe to DhunDetox for daily Indian classical healing music, raga therapy, and mindful sound journeys.

chapters.title: Alap — Free Exploration of Raag Chandrakauns

music_prompt: For Lyria: Compose Raag Chandrakauns at a 70 BPM inner pulse for veena and tabla, designed for deep study and focus. The veena leads with a haunting, slow alap, emphasizing meend glides and holding komal gandhar and komal nishad notes long, with wide silence between phrases. Each note should decay with long, ethereal reverb, contributing to a deeply meditative atmosphere. The tabla should enter softly and subtly after approximately 90 seconds, providing a sparse, deliberate rhythm without fills or sudden dynamics. The overall feel should embody laser-focus, where each musical phrase feels like a thought completing itself – no distraction, no hurry, no buildup, and no climax. The recording should have a warm, analog quality, with a loop-friendly structure. Absolutely no Western instruments, no elaborate percussion fills, and no abrupt changes in dynamics are to be included.
image_prompt: A richly detailed Indian painting in the style of Mughal miniatures fused with Madhubani folk art, rendered with warm earthy pigments on aged parchment, showcasing intricate crosshatching and stippling. The composition centers on a serene scholar, eyes closed in deep focus, seated on a lotus-patterned mat in a moonlit corner, flanked by a prominent veena and tabla. A single oil lamp casts a warm, golden-orange glow, illuminating the space. Outside a geometrically latticed window, a majestic full moon glows softly in a deep indigo and violet sky. A peacock, symbolizing contemplation, is perched gracefully on the windowsill, its gaze serene. From the veena and tabla, visible wisps of shimmering, golden music gently unfurl and ascend, dissolving into the quiet midnight air and intertwining with subtle light rays from the moon. The entire scene is framed by an ornate, hand-painted border featuring intricate fish motifs and stylized botanical elements. The palette is dominated by deep navy, profound violet, and saffron, accented by the soft gold of the lamp and moonlight, creating an atmosphere of absolute stillness and profound concentration.
video_prompt:
Animate this image as a seamless loop video.
Camera completely static and locked, no movement.

Only animate these subtle elements:
- Oil lamp flame flickering gently (5%)
- Full moon glow subtly pulsing (2%)
- Shimmering, golden music wisps gently unfurling and ascending
- Subtle smoke rising from the oil lamp (80%)
- Ornate botanical border leaves swaying softly (3%)

Everything else completely frozen and still:
Scholar, veena, tabla, lotus-patterned mat, geometric window, peacock, fish border.

First frame and last frame must be identical.
No brightness change. No color shift. No flicker.
8 second seamless loop.
Madhubani folk art style preserved exactly.
A timeless scene of tranquil, focused midnight introspection.
